methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Elroy Air is a Series B stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by David Merrill and Clint Cope. The valuation universe for frontier autonomous platforms consists of seven private peers including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Manna Aero, and Asylon Robotics, with no public comparables available in the narrow sector.

Given sparse peer data at equivalent stage and valuation tier, only the stage-progression method was applicable. Using the $400M last post-money valuation as a base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage progression yields $760M. This reflects expected capital appreciation between funding rounds absent market-moving operational inflection points.

The triangulated range supports $646M to $874M, with $760M as the median point. This analysis carries material uncertainty stemming from the absence of sufficiently comparable same-stage peers with disclosed valuations. The narrow peer set and lack of public market benchmarks limit anchoring precision. Key upside or downside drivers would include execution on autonomous vehicle autonomy milestones, customer traction, and regulatory pathway clarity—factors not quantifiable from available data. The $400M post-money should be viewed as a floor, with upside conditioned on demonstrable progress in operations and market validation since that round close.
